project.name: paper_claw
project.title: Paper Claw - CUA Benchmark Edition
project.description: Daily arXiv digest for Computer Use Agent benchmarks

watched_authors._comment: Papers by any of these people are ALWAYS included, pinned in a 'Authors you follow' section at the top, regardless of topic score. Each entry has a canonical 'name' (shown in the email) and 'aliases' (every name string to match against arXiv author lists, case-insensitive). Use the form the author uses on Google Scholar as the canonical name. Edit this list freely.

relevance.profile: I am a researcher at CMU focused on autonomous AI agents, with a primary interest in Computer Use Agents (CUA): agents that operate computers, GUIs, web browsers, desktops, and mobile devices by perceiving screens and taking UI actions. My specific interests, in rough priority order: (1) CUA / GUI agent BENCHMARKS and EVALUATION — new environments, tasks, eval protocols, and methodology for measuring agent capability (e.g. OSWorld, WebArena, Mind2Web style work); (2) CUA / GUI agent METHODS — GUI grounding, screen/UI understanding, web/desktop/OS/browser/mobile agents, action planning and policy for controlling interfaces; (3) AGENT LEARNING & ARCHITECTURE — reinforcement learning for agents, agent training/fine-tuning, multi-agent systems, tool use, planning, and novel agent architectures; (4) AGENT MEMORY — long-term/episodic memory, memory-augmented and self-evolving agents; (5) HUMAN-AGENT INTERACTION — how people supervise, collaborate with, or interface with autonomous agents; (6) OTHER genuinely novel or surprising agent research that an agents researcher would find exciting. I am NOT interested in: papers with no connection to autonomous agents, pure vision/speech/NLP work that doesn't involve agents, generic LLM training or theory unrelated to agency, or papers that merely mention 'agent' in passing without agents being central.

deep_read._comment: The top-scoring papers each get a separate, deeper LLM pass (deep_read_model) that reads the abstract closely and writes a fuller analysis. The rest get the concise batched summary.

huggingface._comment: Pull Hugging Face Daily Papers (community-curated, with upvotes) as both a popularity-boost signal and an extra source of papers the arXiv RSS feed missed. upvote_boost adds up to a few relevance points scaled by log(upvotes); papers on HF Daily get a 🔥 badge.
